title: pinus cembroides subsp. johannis
intro: Pinus cembroides subsp. johannis, also known as the Mexican pinyon, is a subspecies of pine tree native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Pinus cembroides subsp. johannis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and even exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and topical applications for medicinal purposes.
